title: Capital Markets Day 2026 : Capgemini idéalement positionné pour capter la pleine valeur de la révolution de l’IA agentique
author: Capgemini
description: Capgemini unveiled its strategic roadmap and financial ambitions through 2028, positioning itself as a key partner for enterprises deploying agentic AI at scale. The company targets revenue CAGR of 5.5-7.5% (2025-2028), operating margin expansion of 130-150 basis points, and cumulative organic free cash flow exceeding €6 billion (2026-2028). The strategy focuses on five value drivers: modernizing legacy technology, implementing new agentic tech stacks, establishing AI governance control planes, agentifying products/services, and transforming enterprise operations.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company announced ambitious growth targets (5.5-7.5% CAGR), significant margin expansion (130-150 bps), strong free cash flow generation (€6B+), and strategic positioning in the high-growth agentic AI market. Leadership expressed confidence in capturing substantial value from enterprise AI transformation. Introduction of improved profitability metrics and clear execution roadmap support positive outlook.

title: 2026 Capital Markets Day: Capgemini poised to capture the full value of the Agentic AI revolution
author: Capgemini
description: Capgemini announced its 2028 financial ambition and strategic direction at its Capital Markets Day, positioning itself to capitalize on the agentic AI revolution. The Group targets a 2025-2028 revenue CAGR of +5.5% to +7.5% (with ~2 points from M&A), plans to increase operating profit margin by 130-150 basis points to reach 12.1%-12.3%, and aims to generate cumulative organic free cash flow above €6bn over 2026-2028. The company introduced a new comprehensive profitability metric (Adjusted Operating Profit) and outlined five AI-driven value pools to drive growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Capgemini announced ambitious growth targets with 5.5%-7.5% revenue CAGR through 2028, significant margin expansion of 130-150 bps, and strong free cash flow generation above €6bn. The company positions itself as a leader in the emerging agentic AI market with unique end-to-end capabilities, reinforced by strategic acquisitions (WNS, Altran) and a proven track record in complex transformations. Management expressed confidence in capturing substantial value from the AI revolution.

title: Capgemini SE: Shareholders' Meeting of May 20, 2026
author: Not Specified
description: Capgemini SE held its Shareholders' Meeting on May 20, 2026, approving a dividend of €3.40 per share for 2025, board member renewals, and new director appointments. The company appointed Patrick Pouyanné as Lead Independent Director and added two new independent directors: Véronique Weill and Luc Rémont. The Board now comprises 15 directors with 83% independence and 50% women representation.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company approved a healthy dividend payout (€3.40 per share), maintained strong governance with separated Chairman and CEO roles, achieved high board independence (83%), and strengthened diversity with 50% women directors. The appointment of experienced external directors and stable leadership succession planning indicate positive organizational health and shareholder confidence.

title: Capgemini SE : Assemblée Générale du 20 mai 2026
author: Capgemini Se
description: Capgemini's shareholders approved all resolutions at their May 20, 2026 General Assembly, including a €3.40 dividend per share for 2025. The board approved the reappointment of Paul Hermelin as President and made several governance changes, including appointing two new independent administrators (Véronique Weill and Luc Rémont) and new committee leadership. The board now comprises 15 members with 83% independent directors and 50% women.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company successfully held its AGM with unanimous approval of all resolutions, approved a healthy dividend payout (€3.40 per share), maintained stable leadership with Paul Hermelin's reappointment, and strengthened board composition with experienced independent directors. The company also demonstrates strong governance practices with high independent director representation (83%) and gender diversity (50% women), exceeding regulatory requirements ahead of 2027 deadline.

title: Capgemini SE: Notice of publication of a bond prospectus
author: Not Specified
description: Capgemini announced the successful placement of an €800 million bond issuance with a 7-year maturity and 3.875% coupon. The prospectus received visa number 26-131 from the French financial authority (AMF) and is available on the company and AMF websites.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company successfully completed a significant €800 million bond placement, indicating strong market confidence in Capgemini's creditworthiness and financial stability. This capital raising demonstrates investor appetite for the company's debt securities and provides financial flexibility for operations and strategic initiatives.
